pr.title: Split out lbarg and lbmemory from code_8000F9F8
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Mechanical assembly organization PR that split a large block of lb assembly out of asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s into two dedicated assembly translation units, asm/melee/lb/lbarq.s and asm/melee/lb/lbmemory.s, and added both new objects to obj_files.mk. The change appears to preserve existing labels and raw assembly while relocating module-specific text/data, including source-name strings for lbarq.c and lbmemory.c, making later decompilation work more modular.

key_files.role: source assembly file reduced by removing the lbarq/lbmemory blocks
new dedicated assembly file for the lbarq.c-associated block
key_files.evidence: 274 added lines; contains the string "lbarq.c" and ARQ-related symbols/calls such as ARQPostRequest in the moved block

key_files.role: new dedicated assembly file for the lbmemory.c-associated block
key_files.evidence: 815 added lines; contains the string "lbmemory.c" and memory-management/assert strings such as "_p(free_heap)" and "p->size"

key_files.role: build list updated to compile/link the new assembly objects

what_changed: Removed a contiguous assembly/data region from asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s, starting after lbl_80014AA8 and covering functions and labels around 80014ABC through the 800150xx region in the provided excerpt.
Created asm/melee/lb/lbarq.s with 274 lines of moved assembly and associated data, including the source filename string "lbarq.c" and sdata labels lbl_804D3778/lbl_804D3780.
Created asm/melee/lb/lbmemory.s with 815 lines of moved assembly and associated data, including the source filename string "lbmemory.c" and rodata/assert strings.
Updated obj_files.mk to include lbarq.s.o and lbmemory.s.o in TEXT_O_FILES, preserving participation in the link.
smart_moves: Kept the split mechanical: no evidence of symbol renaming or C conversion in this PR, which reduces matching risk when reorganizing a large assembly blob.
Moved module-specific string/data labels together with the corresponding text blocks instead of leaving source-name/assert strings in the old aggregate file.
Added the new object files adjacent to code_8000F9F8.s.o in obj_files.mk, a conservative build-order choice for a file split where address/link ordering may matter.
Used dedicated filenames matching the embedded source strings, making the assembly layout reflect the likely original translation units and improving searchability for future decomp work.
decomp_lessons: Before decompiling an oversized aggregate assembly file, split obvious original translation units into their own asm files when source filename strings and coherent function ranges identify them.
When moving assembly between files, carry over nearby rodata/sdata labels, .asciz strings, and .balign directives with the code that references them.
A safe module split can be valuable even without decompiling functions: it reduces file size, improves ownership boundaries, and creates a clearer target for later C reconstruction.
After any asm split, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the build continues to link the relocated symbols.
naming_conventions: New assembly files use lower-case lb module names under asm/melee/lb/: lbarq.s and lbmemory.s.
The new file names correspond to embedded original source filename strings: "lbarq.c" and "lbmemory.c".
Existing placeholder symbols were preserved, including func_80014ABC, func_80014BD0, func_80014D2C, func_80014E24, func_80014EEC, func_80014F7C, func_80014FC8 and lbl_* data/control labels.
The PR title says "lbarg", but the changed file and embedded string evidence indicate the module is "lbarq".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed a raw assembly relocation rather than a semantic rewrite, preserving instruction bytes/comments and global labels.
Included macros.inc at the top of new assembly files, consistent with standalone asm file setup.
Preserved alignment-sensitive data directives such as .balign along with .asciz strings in the destination modules.
Maintained object-file build inclusion through TEXT_O_FILES so the split modules continue to be linked.
searchable_terms: Split out lbarg and lbmemory from code_8000F9F8
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/lb/lbarq.s into src/melee/lb/lbarq.c.
Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/lb/lbmemory.s into src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c.
Search for follow-up symbol naming of func_80014ABC through func_80014FC8.
Check whether the title typo "lbarg" was ever corrected or repeated in related PRs.
